_meta.review_notes: Heuristic auto-review 2026-04-22: verified source URLs match a .gov/official domain where possible, rejected items matching industry-convention/proposed-not-enacted/unverified-rate patterns. Remaining entries require secondary admin approval before draft->active flip.

notes: 1. The official Code des Impôts for French Polynesia is hosted on Lexpol (lexpol.cloud.pf), which was not directly accessible. Most rates confirmed via the DICP FAQ/official pages and cross-referenced against Airbnb's professional tax guides prepared by independent third-party firms. 2. The taxe de séjour rates (150 XPF classified / 50 XPF unclassified) are set by municipal council deliberation within the band limits set by the 1997 arrêté (80–200 XPF for classified, per the 2011 tariff presentation). The 150 XPF figure for Papeete is confirmed by travel trade sources but no direct municipal deliberation text was found. 3. The taxe pour le développement touristique exact rate schedule for classified hotels in Papeete specifically was not found in primary legislation – range quoted (50–2,000 XPF/person/night territory-wide) varies significantly by island and hotel category. A more conservative range of 20–200 XPF is cited in the 2025 Airbnb guide for furnished rentals. 4. The 1% TVA for remote archipelagos (Tuamotu-Gambier, Marquises, Australes), enacted by the Assemblée de Polynésie française and effective July 1, 2026, explicitly excludes the Société archipelago (including Tahiti/Papeete) and is therefore not reported as applicable to this jurisdiction. 5. The effective_start dates for tourism taxes are approximate; exact enactment dates could not be confirmed from accessible sources.

rates.source_quote: 50 xpf par jour et par personne dans les hôtels non classés et autres établissements équivalents

rates.legal_reference: Code des Impôts de Polynésie française – taxe pour le développement touristique
rates.source_quote: Tourism tax will be due per night and per person. The amount of the tax depends on the location of your property and may vary between 20F.CFP/night/pers and 200 F.CFP.

tiers.description: Range for furnished rental / non-classified accommodation, varies by location and accommodation category
Range for classified hotels and cruise vessels, varies by island and accommodation category

rates.legal_reference: Code des Impôts de Polynésie française – taxe pour le développement de la croisière
rates.source_quote: La taxe pour le développement de la croisière s'applique aux passagers des navires de croisière qui opèrent habituellement ou occasionnellement en Polynésie française. Elle est de 500 XPF par passager et par escale.

rules.description: Children under 12 years of age are exempt from the municipal taxe de séjour (stay tax) in all Polynesian communes that levy the tax, including Papeete.
rules.legal_reference: Code des Communes de Polynésie française art. L.233-29 à L.233-45; Arrêté n°0066/MAC du 29 janvier 1997
rules.name: Children Under 12 – Taxe de Séjour Exemption
rules.source_quote: Les enfants de moins de 12 ans ne sont pas assujettis à cette taxe.

rules.description: The taxe de séjour is not levied by all communes in French Polynesia. It is only collected in communes that have formally instituted it by municipal council deliberation. Papeete is among the communes confirmed as levying this tax.
rules.legal_reference: Code des Communes de Polynésie française art. L.233-29 à L.233-45
rules.name: Taxe de Séjour – Papeete Municipality Only
rules.source_quote: Taxe de séjour (uniquement perçue à Papeete, Faa'a, Punaauia, Moorea-Maiao, Bora Bora, Huahine, Rangiroa, Mataiva et Tikehau).

rules.description: The territorial tourism development tax (taxe pour le développement touristique) applies only to classified hotel rooms and cruise ship berths. Unclassified establishments and furnished rentals are subject to a different, lower rate band under the general municipal stay tax framework.
rules.legal_reference: Code des Impôts de Polynésie française – taxe pour le développement touristique
rules.name: Taxe pour le Développement Touristique – Classified Hotels Only
rules.source_quote: Taxe pour le développement touristique sur les chambres d'hôtels classées et les croisières

rules.description: Hotel accommodation services (hébergement dans les établissements hôteliers), including cruise and charter vessels, benefit from the reduced 5% TVA rate rather than the standard 13% or 16% rates. This applies throughout the Société archipelago including Papeete/Tahiti.
rules.legal_reference: Code des Impôts de Polynésie française – taux réduit de TVA à 5%
rules.name: VAT 5% – Hotel Accommodation Services
rules.source_quote: Ce taux s'applique également aux prestations de services telles que l'hébergement hôtelier (navires de croisière et de charter compris

action.description: Degressive rate applies – exact brackets not publicly specified

rules.description: The 500 XPF/passenger/port-call cruise development tax includes a degressive (declining scale) system for cruise lines operating frequently in French Polynesia.
rules.legal_reference: Code des Impôts de Polynésie française – taxe pour le développement de la croisière
rules.name: Cruise Ship Development Tax – Degressive Rate for Frequent Operators
rules.source_quote: Un système dégressif est prévu en faveur des navires qui opèrent durablement en Polynésie française.

summary: Comprehensive research across 10+ sources including the official DICP tax authority (impot-polynesie.gov.pf), the French Polynesia High Commissariat, Airbnb's 2021/2023/2024/2025 French Polynesia tax guides, and travel trade sources. Four distinct accommodation-related taxes were identified for Tahiti/Papeete: (1) A 5% reduced TVA (VAT) rate on hotel accommodation confirmed by the official DICP FAQ; (2) A municipal taxe de séjour (stay tax) of 150 XPF/person/night for classified hotels and 50 XPF/person/night for unclassified establishments, levied by Papeete and neighboring communes; (3) A territorial taxe pour le développement touristique on classified hotel rooms and cruises (20–200 XPF range for furnished rentals per Airbnb 2025 guide; broader 50–2,000 XPF range for all classified hotels per tourism sources); (4) A cruise ship development tax of 500 XPF/passenger/port call. No rates existed in our database, so all are reported as 'new'. The 1% TVA for remote archipelagos (effective July 1, 2026) explicitly does NOT apply to Papeete/Tahiti (Société archipelago) and is therefore not reported. Municipal taxe de séjour rates are set by each commune's deliberation within national tariff bands; Papeete-specific rates are confirmed at 150 XPF (classified) and 50 XPF (unclassified). Overall confidence is moderate due to the lack of a fully accessible live Code des Impôts rate schedule and the fact that some sources are travel trade rather than primary legislation.
